summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Infæmous Freight MVP with all extended features. Backend APIs are 100% functional including calculator, analytics, payments, messaging with AI summarization. Frontend has Select component validation issues but core functionality works.

ui_bugs.issue: Select.Item components have empty string value props causing React validation errors
ui_bugs.selector: All dropdown selects (Calculator, Lane Preferences, etc.)

ui_bugs.issue: Calculate button remains disabled even when states are selected due to Select validation errors
Page fails to load due to Select component errors in state dropdowns

integration_issues.issue: Frontend calculator form has Select validation preventing rate calculation despite working backend API

passed_tests: Backend API health endpoints (100% success)
User registration and login APIs
Load CRUD operations via API
Bidding system APIs
Booking/assignment APIs
Messaging system APIs
AI thread summarization (OpenAI integration working)
Rate calculator API (CA to TX calculation working)
Premium packages API (verified carrier, boost packages)
Analytics API (market data, user dashboard)
Lane preferences API (add/delete/match loads)
Homepage loads with navbar and Tools dropdown
User login flow (carrier2@test.com authentication)
Analytics page shows market data and user stats
Loads page displays available loads with filtering
Load detail pages accessible with proper data
Messages page loads with appropriate empty state
Premium Services page shows packages with purchase options
Profile page loads with editable form and verification status
Dashboard accessible via navigation
Tools dropdown navigation to Calculator and Analytics
Navigation between all major pages working
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix Select component validation errors by ensuring all SelectItem components have non-empty string values. This is preventing Rate Calculator and Lane Preferences from working properly. The backend APIs are all functional, so this is purely a frontend UI component issue.
